# Fix Multi-Select Drag — All Selected Widgets Must Move Together

## Problem

When multiple widgets are selected (A,B,C,D,E) and you drag one (D), only D moves visually during the drag. The peers should follow in real-time.

## Root Cause

Two bugs in `CanvasPage.jsx`:

1. **`parseTranslate` reads the wrong CSS property.** Neodrag positions elements via `style.transform: translate3d(...)`, but `parseTranslate()` reads `style.translate` — a separate CSS property that is always empty. This means `dx/dy` is always 0 during the drag, so peer widgets never move.

2. **`clearDragPreview` doesn't reset peer DOM styles.** After the drag, peers retain any `style.translate` set during the preview, causing visual offset after React re-renders with new positions. (Latent bug — currently not triggered because of bug #1.)

Additionally, there's a UX issue where the `onClick` handler on the widget wrapper replaces the multi-selection with a single widget after a completed drag, collapsing the selection.

## Approach

**Fix the drag preview** by passing position data from the onDrag/onDragStart callbacks (which already provide `{x, y}`) instead of reading from the DOM. Apply the delta to peers via `style.translate` (which composes on top of their existing `style.transform` from neodrag).

**Fix the style cleanup** by resetting `style.translate = ''` on all peer articles in `clearDragPreview`.

**Fix selection preservation** by tracking whether a drag just occurred and skipping the onClick-based selection change in that case.

## Files to Change

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `packages/react/src/canvas/CanvasPage.jsx` | Rewrite `handleItemDragStart`, `handleItemDrag`, `clearDragPreview`; add drag-completed flag to skip post-drag selection reset |

## Steps

1. Replace `parseTranslate` + DOM reading with position-based tracking:
   - `handleItemDragStart(dragId, position)` — store `position` as drag start (already passed by tiny-canvas)
   - `handleItemDrag(dragId, position)` — compute delta from stored start, apply to peers via `style.translate`
2. Fix `clearDragPreview` to reset `style.translate = ''` on all peer articles
3. Add a `justDraggedRef` flag set in `handleItemDragEnd` and cleared in `requestAnimationFrame`, used by the onClick handler to skip selection change after a multi-drag
4. Update tests in `CanvasPage.multiselect.test.jsx`

## Edge Cases & Risks

- Single-widget drag must remain unaffected (guard: `ids.size <= 1`)
- JSX-sourced component widgets also go through the same drag path — they should work the same
- Snap-to-grid during drag should only affect the dragged widget; peers follow by delta
- After drag, selection must remain intact (all 5 still selected)
